title: Binary file uploads as 0 bytes in HTTP Request node
symptom: When attempting to upload a binary file using the HTTP Request node, the file uploads as 0 bytes instead of the expected size.
applies_when: File is being handled by a previous node
not_this_if: File is not being processed by a node that outputs binary data
root_cause: The HTTP Request node requires proper mapping of binary data to upload files correctly. If the binary data is not set up correctly or the property name does not match, it results in uploading an empty file.
fix_steps: Ensure the file is being handled by a node that outputs binary data, like 'Read Binary File'.
In the HTTP Request node, set 'Send Binary Data' to true.
Specify the correct binary property name in the 'Binary Property' field.
Set the HTTP method to PUT and provide the correct URL.
Use Basic Auth with the appropriate credentials.
agent_instruction: Check the binary data mapping in the HTTP Request node and ensure the file is correctly processed by a previous node.
